Class BoardView

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.layout.Pane
com.cluegame.gui.BoardView
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class BoardView extends javafx.scene.layout.Pane
Displays the Clue! board image with player tokens drawn on a Canvas overlay. The board scales responsively to fit the allocated layout space while preserving its aspect ratio. Token positions are recalculated on each resize. Supports highlighting valid move squares and click-to-move interaction. Original board dimensions: 1296x1226 pixels, 24 columns x 25 rows.
Author:
Thanh Shaw

  • Constructor Details

    • BoardView

      public BoardView(Game game)
      Creates a BoardView that loads the board image and sets up the token overlay canvas. The board automatically scales to fit whatever space it is given by its parent layout.
      Parameters:
      game - the Game instance containing players and board data

  • Method Details

    • getTokenColour

      private static javafx.scene.paint.Color getTokenColour(String token)
      Returns the display colour for a player based on their character token.
      Parameters:
      token - the short token name (e.g. "Scarlett")
      Returns:
      the colour for that character

    • setClickListener

      public void setClickListener(BiConsumer<Integer,Integer> listener)
      Sets a listener that is called when the user clicks a board cell. The listener receives the grid row and column that was clicked.
      Parameters:
      listener - callback accepting (row, col)

    • setHighlights

      public void setHighlights(List<int[]> squares)
      Sets the list of squares to highlight as valid move destinations. Triggers a redraw of the overlay.
      Parameters:
      squares - list of {row, col} arrays to highlight

    • clearHighlights

      public void clearHighlights()
      Clears all highlighted squares and redraws.

    • onCanvasClicked

      private void onCanvasClicked(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event)
      Handles a mouse click on the canvas. Converts pixel coordinates to grid row/col using the scaled grid offset, and notifies the click listener. Rejects clicks outside the grid area.

    • layoutChildren

      protected void layoutChildren()
      Called by JavaFX during layout. Scales the board image and canvas to fit within the actual allocated width and height, centered.
      Overrides:
      layoutChildren in class javafx.scene.Parent

    • drawOverlay

      public void drawOverlay()
      Draws the full canvas overlay: highlighted squares then player tokens. All positions use the scaled grid offset so elements align with the actual board cells in the image.

    • drawDebugOverlay

      private void drawDebugOverlay(javafx.scene.canvas.GraphicsContext gc, double scale, double scaledGridLeft, double scaledGridTop, double scaledCellW, double scaledCellH)
      Draws the debug grid overlay showing cell types from Board.java. Grid lines in grey, doors outlined in cyan, blocked in red, rooms in a subtle tint, and row/col labels on corridor cells.

    • getRoomTokenPosition

      private double[] getRoomTokenPosition(Player player, int playerIndex, double sgl, double sgt, double scw, double sch)
      Returns the pixel position for a player's token inside their room. Each player gets a distinct offset from the room center so tokens don't overlap.
      Parameters:
      player - the player in a room
      playerIndex - the player's index in the players list
      sgl - scaled grid left
      sgt - scaled grid top
      scw - scaled cell width
      sch - scaled cell height
      Returns:
      {x, y} pixel position

    • drawTokens

      public void drawTokens()
      Redraws tokens and highlights. Convenience method for external callers.
